{{Quote|Whatever happens to me, they can't say I didn't warn them.|Lieutenant Kiviett|<ref name="ISP" />}}
'''Kiviett''' was a [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] and [[Planetologist/Legends|planetologist]] who served in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Survey Team]] [[IX3244-B]] as second-in-command to [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Toss]]. IX3244-B was tasked with finding the construction site for the Imperial [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]], discovering the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]], which Kiviett had issues with for a [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] construction site. His concerns were only compounded when he and others in the team went to Endor and encountered the [[Moon/Legends|moon's]] [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] [[Species/Legends|species]], who led them in circles and made them unable to reach the Ewoks' [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]]. Kiviett's concerns were dismissed by Toss, although the Ewoks were ultimately essential to the Imperial defeat in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY's]] [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. Taken [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]] by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], Kiviett offered to join them as a planetologist.

==Biography==
===Surveying Endor===
{{Quote|We didn't make it to within a kilometer of the tree house city.|Kiviett, on how he and others in IX3244-B failed to reach Bright Tree Village|<ref name="ISP" />}}
Kiviett was a [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] and [[Planetologist/Legends|planetologist]] within the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. A member of the Empire's [[Survey Team]] [[IX3244-B]], Kiviett was second-in-command, reporting to [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Toss]]. At some point<ref name="ISP" /> after [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY's]] [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> and by [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">[[Mission 759/B]], which is described in {{WEGCite|story=Imperial Scouting Party|book=Galaxy Guide 5: Return of the Jedi}}, occurred before [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Pfilbee Jhorn]]'s expedition to the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]], as detailed in ''[[The Illustrated Star Wars Universe]]'', when the latter mentioned being sent to the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] after [[Survey Team]] [[IX3244-B]]'s expedition there. Jhorn mentions his expedition to Endor in [[Star Wars Galaxies|''Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided'']], which takes place in [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]] per the reasoning [[Template:SWG timeline|here]]. From this reasoning, both IX3244-B and Jhorn's expeditions must have therefore occurred by that [[Standard year/Legends|year]].</ref> Imperial Survey Teams were assigned to find a construction site for the Empire's [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]].<ref name="ISP" />

[[File:VictoryOnEndor.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Kiviett was afraid of the Ewoks after he and others in IX3244-B encountered the species.]]
For the team's [[Mission 759/B]],<ref name="SWS">{{WEGCite|story=Report to Imperial Command|book=The Star Wars Sourcebook}}</ref> IX3244-B visited several [[Planet/Legends|planets]] before their survey [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]] found the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. [[Orbit/Legends|Orbital]] scans revealed that Endor was capable of sustaining [[Human/Legends|Human]] life. Combined with viewing the [[Moon/Legends|moon's]] remote location as good for a construction site, [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] perceived that none of Endor's indigenous life forms could pose a risk for the Empire's presence. Kiviett believed a barren rock with low [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] and a [[Poison/Legends|poisonous]] [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] would be better, with no indigenous [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] potentially intervening and no cover for any hostile [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] [[Commando/Legends|commando]] operations. However, Toss and Vader never asked for Kiviett's opinion, believing no indigenous [[Species/Legends|species]] would be that much a risk and that it would be a waste of [[Time/Legends|time]] to build [[Life support/Legends|life-support]] systems and domes for the [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generator]] construction site.<ref name="ISP" />

While IX3244-B's frigate remained in Endor's [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]], further scans showed many primitive life forms on the moon. Toss sent Kiviett and others from the team to the moon to investigate, with Kiviett hearing the captain make a [[holoreport]] about how Endor was looking like a great location for the shield generator before the team left. After their [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] landed on Endor, Kiviett and the other team members attempted to reach<ref name="ISP" /> the native [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks']] [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]].<ref name="FF">{{FactFile|11|END 7-10|The Battle of Endor}}</ref> The Ewoks, however, led them in circles and confused even the team's best [[Scout/Legends|scouts]]. At one point, the Ewoks beating their [[Drum/Legends|drums]] further spooked the team members, who fired their [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]] in an attempt to get the music to stop. Ultimately wandering for more than two [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] and unable to reach within a [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometer]] of the Ewok village, Kiviett and the others hastily retreated back to the frigate.<ref name="ISP" />

===Return to the frigate and eventual capture by the Alliance===
{{Quote|Just keep me away from those Ewoks, okay? They make me very nervous.|Kiviett, expressing his fear of the Ewoks|<ref name="ISP" />}}
Upon returning to the frigate, Kiviett reported that Endor's creatures were a potential problem in need of further study. However, Toss ignored the report and dismissed Kiviett's suggestion to apply a [[Forest/Legends|forest]] camouflage to all the Imperial vehicles and [[Armor/Legends|armor]].<ref name="ISP" />

[[File:PfilbeeJhorn2-ISWUp114.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Sergeant Pfilbee Jhorn, who led a second Survey Team sent to Endor as a result of Kiviett's team making superficial reports]]
Because [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] was displeased with superficial reports made by Kiviett's team, he assigned a second team led by [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Pfilbee Jhorn]] to go to Endor. Jhorn, who was also unhappy with IX3244-B's reports, viewed Kiviett and the others as disgraces to the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] for failing to mention the presence of such dangers as [[Gorax/Legends|Gorax]], [[Condor dragon/Legends|condor dragons]], and [[Sanyassan/Legends|Sanyassan]] Marauders<ref name="The Illustrated Star Wars Universe">''[[The Illustrated Star Wars Universe]]''</ref>—although Kiviett's report, which was ignored by Toss, indeed discussed the need to further study Endor's creatures.<ref name="ISP" />

In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="FF" /> Kiviett was still serving as IX3244-B's second-in-command when the remote moon's Ewoks helped the Alliance defeat the Empire in the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. He was taken [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]] by the Alliance and spoke to Alliance [[Historian/Legends|historian]] [[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al]] about his time with IX3244-B during Endor's initial discovery. Kiviett also offered to join the Alliance as a planetologist.<ref name="ISP" /> At another point after the Battle of Endor, Na'al had also interviewed the Ewok [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Wystri Warrick]], publishing the interview where the Ewok recounted his own personal experiences with Kiviett and IX3244-B's attempt to reach Bright Tree Village, along with later encounters with the Empire and defeating the Empire in the Battle of Endor.<ref name="GG5">{{WEGCite|story=The Ewok Defenses|book=Galaxy Guide 5: Return of the Jedi}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|This whole Ewok thing … I saw it coming from the very beginning. It's not ''my'' fault.|Kiviett, about the Empire's defeat|<ref name="ISP" />}}
[[File:VorenNaal.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Voren Na'al, to whom Kiviett spoke about joining the Alliance]]
Kiviett disagreed with Toss and Vader's assessments of Endor as a good location for the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] shield generator, although his opinions were not asked for. He found it amusing to look back at how he did not register that the sound of the Imperial shuttle landing on Endor alone was heard by the Ewoks, when he and the others attempted to not be heard. Being a planetologist, Kiviett set his [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] to the [[Stun/Legends|stun]] setting and instructed the [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] with him to do the same. He also compared himself and his group to the Ewoks as three men in light armor and two stormtroopers, being unable to make it to within a kilometer of the primitive creatures' village.<ref name="ISP" />

After retreating along with the others to the shuttle, Kiviett's embarrassment was only compounded by Toss's laughter. He viewed Toss as a blind fool and viewed the latter ignoring his concerns as being eager to take credit for discovering the new Death Star construction site. Kiviett was not surprised that the Ewoks played a role in the Empire's defeat. He believed the Empire was doomed but was also big and powerful and that the Alliance had more work to do before their victory was complete. Kiviett ultimately offered to join the Alliance as a planetologist, although he requested to be kept away from the Ewoks.<ref name="ISP" />

==Behind the scenes==
Kiviett was created by author [[Michael Stern]] for "[[Imperial Scouting Party]]," a story released in [[1990]] for ''[[Galaxy Guide 5: Return of the Jedi]]''. As the [[in-universe]] narrator being interviewed by Voren Na'al in that story,<ref name="ISP" /> the IX3244-B team was also mentioned in "[[The Ewok Defenses]]," another narrative published in an earlier section of the book.<ref name="GG5" />
